Package de.uka.ilkd.key.util

Class MiscTools

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.MiscTools
public final class MiscTools extends Object
Collection of some common, stateless functionality. Stolen from the weissInvariants side branch.

  • Method Summary

    Method
    Description
    static List<LocationVariable>
    applicableHeapContexts(Modality.JavaModalityKind modalityKind, Services services)
    Returns the applicable heap contexts out of the currently available set of three contexts: The normal heap, the saved heap (transaction), and the permission heap.

  • Method Details

    • getSpecForTermWithLoopStmt

      public static @Nullable LoopSpecification getSpecForTermWithLoopStmt(Term loopTerm, Services services)
      Returns the LoopSpecification for the program in the given term, the active statement of which has to be a loop statement. Returns an empty Optional if there is no specification for that statement. Asserts that there is indeed a Java block in the term which has as active statement a loop statement, thus throws an AssertionError if not or otherwise results in undefined behavior in that case.
      Parameters:
      loopTerm - The term for which to return the LoopSpecification.
      Returns:
      The LoopSpecification for the loop statement in the given term or an empty optional if there is no specified invariant for the loop.

    • isPermissions

      public static boolean isPermissions(Services services)
      Parameters:
      services - The Services object.
      Returns:
      true iff the given Services object is associated to a Profile with permissions.

    • isTransaction

      public static boolean isTransaction(Modality.JavaModalityKind modalityKind)
      Checks whether the given Modality.JavaModalityKind is a transaction modality.
      Parameters:
      modalityKind - The modality to check.
      Returns:
      true iff the given Modality.JavaModalityKind is a transaction modality.

    • applicableHeapContexts

      public static List<LocationVariable> applicableHeapContexts(Modality.JavaModalityKind modalityKind, Services services)
      Returns the applicable heap contexts out of the currently available set of three contexts: The normal heap, the saved heap (transaction), and the permission heap.
      Parameters:
      modalityKind - The current modality (checked for transaction).
      services - The Services object (for HeapLDT and for checking whether we're in the permissions profile).
      Returns:
      The list of the applicable heaps for the given scenario.

    • getSelf

      public static ProgramVariable getSelf(MethodFrame mf)

    • getSelfTerm

      public static Term getSelfTerm(MethodFrame mf, Services services)
      Returns the receiver term of the passed method frame, or null if the frame belongs to a static method.
      Parameters:
      mf - a method frame.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      the receiver term of the passed method frame, or null if the frame belongs to a static method.

      pe - a program element.
      services - services.
      Returns:
      all variables read in the specified program element, excluding newly declared variables.

      services - services.
      Returns:
      all variables changed in the specified program element, excluding newly declared variables.

    • collectObservers

      public static ImmutableSet<Pair<Sort,IObserverFunction>> collectObservers(Term t)
      Recursively collect all observers for this term including all of its sub terms.
      Parameters:
      t - the term for which we want to collect the observer functions.
      Returns:
      the observers as a set of pairs with sorts and according observers

    • concat

      public static <S, T extends S> S[] concat(S[] s1, T[] s2)
      Concatenates two arrays.
      Type Parameters:
      S - type o array s1 and of result array.
      T - type of array s2.
      Parameters:
      s1 - an array.
      s2 - another array.
      Returns:
      the concatenation of both arrays.

    • apply

      public static <S, T, U> Map<S,U> apply(Map<S,? extends T> m0, Map<T,U> m1)
      Combine two maps by function application. Values of m0 which are not keys of m1 are dropped. This implementation tries to use the same implementation of Map (provided in Java SE) as m0.
      Type Parameters:
      S - type of m0.
      T - type of m1.
      U - new type of result map indexes.
      Parameters:
      m0 - a map.
      m1 - another map.
      Returns:
      the combination of both maps.

    • makeFilenameRelative

      public static String makeFilenameRelative(String origFilename, String toFilename)
      Returns a filename relative to another one. The second parameter needs to be absolute and is expected to refer to a directory. This method only operates on Strings, not on real files! Note that it treats Strings case-sensitive. The resulting filename always uses UNIX directory delimiters. Raises a RuntimeException if no relative path could be found (may happen on Windows systems).
      Parameters:
      origFilename - a filename.
      toFilename - the name of a parent directory of origFilename.
      Returns:
      origFilename relative to toFilename

    • toValidTacletName

      public static Name toValidTacletName(String s)

    • removeFileExtension

      public static String removeFileExtension(String filename)
      Remove the file extension (.key, .proof) from the given filename.
      Parameters:
      filename - file name
      Returns:
      file name without .key or .proof extension

    • toValidFileName

      public static String toValidFileName(String s)

    • toValidVariableName

      public static Name toValidVariableName(String s)

    • join

      public static String join(Iterable<?> collection, String delimiter)
      Join the string representations of a collection of objects into onw string. The individual elements are separated by a delimiter.

      Object.toString() is used to turn the objects into strings.

      Parameters:
      collection - an arbitrary non-null collection
      delimiter - a non-null string which is put between the elements.
      Returns:
      the concatenation of all string representations separated by the delimiter

    • join

      public static String join(Object[] collection, String delimiter)
      Join the string representations of an array of objects into one string. The individual elements are separated by a delimiter.

      Object.toString() is used to turn the objects into strings.

      Parameters:
      collection - an arbitrary non-null array of objects
      delimiter - a non-null string which is put between the elements.
      Returns:
      the concatenation of all string representations separated by the delimiter

    • filterAlphabetic

      public static String filterAlphabetic(String string)
      Takes a string and returns a string which is potentially shorter and contains a sub-collection of the original characters.

      All alphabetic characters (A-Z and a-z) are copied to the result while all other characters are removed.

      Parameters:
      string - an arbitrary string
      Returns:
      a string which is a sub-structure of the original character sequence

    • containsWholeWord

      public static boolean containsWholeWord(String s, String word)
      Checks whether a string contains another one as a whole word (i.e., separated by white spaces or a semicolon at the end).
      Parameters:
      s - string to search in
      word - string to be searched for
      Returns:
      the answer to the question specified above

    • isJMLComment

      public static boolean isJMLComment(String comment)
      There are different kinds of JML markers. See Section 4.4 "Annotation markers" of the JML reference manual.

    • getRuleDisplayName

      public static String getRuleDisplayName(Node node)

      Returns the display name of the applied rule in the given Node of the proof tree in KeY.

      This method is required for the symbolic execution tree extraction, e.g. used in the Symbolic Execution Tree Debugger.

      Parameters:
      node - The given Node.
      Returns:
      The display name of the applied rule in the given Node or null if no one exists.

    • getRuleDisplayName

      public static String getRuleDisplayName(RuleApp ruleApp)

      Returns the display name of the RuleApp.

      This method is required for the symbolic execution tree extraction, e.g. used in the Symbolic Execution Tree Debugger.

      Parameters:
      ruleApp - The given RuleApp.
      Returns:
      The display name of the RuleApp or null if no one exists.

    • getRuleName

      public static String getRuleName(Node node)

      Returns the name of the applied rule in the given Node of the proof tree in KeY.

      This method is required for the symbolic execution tree extraction, e.g. used in the Symbolic Execution Tree Debugger.

      Parameters:
      node - The given Node.
      Returns:
      The display name of the applied rule in the given Node or null if no one exists.

    • getRuleName

      public static String getRuleName(RuleApp ruleApp)

      Returns the name of the RuleApp.

      This method is required for the symbolic execution tree extraction, e.g. used in the Symbolic Execution Tree Debugger.

      Parameters:
      ruleApp - The given RuleApp.
      Returns:
      The display name of the RuleApp or null if no one exists.

      Parameters:

      Returns:
      The used OneStepSimplifier or null if not available.

      public static OneStepSimplifier findOneStepSimplifier(Profile profile)
      Returns the OneStepSimplifier used in the given Profile.
      Parameters:
      profile - The Profile to get its used OneStepSimplifier.
      Returns:
      The used OneStepSimplifier or null if not available.

      public static ProgramVariable findActualVariable(ProgramVariable originalVar, Node node)
      Returns the actual variable for a given one (this means it returns the renamed variable).
      Parameters:
      node - the Node where to look up the actual variable (result from renaming)
      Returns:
      The renamed variable

      is - a non-null open input stream
      Returns:
      the string created from the input of the stream
      Throws:
      IOException - may occur while reading the stream

    • extractURI

      public static Optional<URI> extractURI(DataLocation loc)
      Tries to extract a valid URI from the given DataLocation.
      Parameters:
      loc - the given DataLocation
      Returns:
      an URI identifying the resource of the DataLocation

    • getZipEntryURI

      public static URI getZipEntryURI(ZipFile zipFile, String entryName) throws IOException
      Creates a URI (that contains a URL) pointing to the entry with the given name inside the given zip file.

      Note: There is an unresolved bug in Java for JarURLs when the jar path contains a directory ending with "!" ("!" should be encoded as "%21", but is not). In this case, the program will crash if trying to open a resource from the url. This will not be fixed until URI supports RFC 3986 (currently, as of 02-2020, it seems like there are no plans for this).
      Workaround: Don't use directory names ending with "!".
      Parameters:
      zipFile - the given zip
      entryName - the entry path relative to the root of the zip
      Returns:
      a zip/jar URI to the entry inside the zip
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs

    • getURIFromTokenSource

      public static @Nullable URI getURIFromTokenSource(TokenSource source)

    • getURIFromTokenSource

      public static @Nullable URI getURIFromTokenSource(String source)

    • parseURL

      public static URL parseURL(String input) throws MalformedURLException
      This method is the central place for parsing a URL from a String. Allowed input formats are:
      • from DataLocation:
        • URLDataLocation: URL:<url>
        • ArchiveDataLocation: ARCHIVE:<filename>?<entry>
        • FileDataLocation: FILE:<filename>
        • SpecDataLocation: <type>://<location>
      • from URL: <scheme>:<scheme_specific_part>
      • from File/Path (in both cases, paths may be relative and/or not normalized!):
        • Unix: /a/b/c
        • Windows: <drive_letter>:\a\b\c\

      A NullPointerException is thrown if null is given. If the input is "", ".", or a relative path in general, the path is resolved against the current working directory (see system property "user.dir") consistently to the behaviour of Paths.get(String, String...).

      Parameters:
      input - the String to convert
      Returns:
      a URL if successful
      Throws:
      MalformedURLException - if the string can not be converted to URL because of an unknown protocol or illegal format
